commonIssues:
Spelling errors in names or differences in address/date of birth across Aadhaar cards, SSC certificates, or marriage affidavits.

How to fix:

  • Correct spelling errors in Aadhaar via UIDAI before applying on the MeeSeva portal.

Slow website, server downtime, or errors when uploading documents to the MeeSeva portal.

How to fix:

  • Compress documents to the required size and apply on the MeeSeva Telangana Portal during non-peak hours.

Difficulty in arranging three witnesses who are available to appear in person with valid identification.

How to fix:

  • Have 3 witnesses ready with original identification. They must accompany you to the registrar's office.

Misunderstanding whether to apply under the Hindu Marriage Act (if both are Hindu) or Special Marriage Act (inter-religious or secular).

How to fix:

  • Apply under Hindu Marriage Act if both are Hindus. Use Special Marriage Act for inter-faith marriages.

Failing to apply within 30 days of the marriage leads to a penalty of ₹50 per month, up to a cap of ₹1000.

How to fix:

  • Register within 30 days. Pay the penalty (up to ₹1000) online if delayed.

Under the Special Marriage Act, a mandatory 30-day notice period is required, which can be extended if objections are raised.

How to fix:

  • For Special Marriage Act, the 30-day notice is mandatory. Follow up at the office after 30 days.

Despite the online application, the Sub-Registrar often requires both partners and witnesses to appear in person, causing scheduling issues.

How to fix:

  • Schedule an appointment and ensure both partners and witnesses are physically present at the Sub-Registrar office.

Discrepancies in the wedding invitation card or photos, or lack of proper documentation, can lead to long delays or rejections.

How to fix:

  • Ensure the wedding invitation and photos are clear and uploaded correctly to prevent verification delays.

Some officials may insist on unnecessary extra documentation, leading to harassment.

How to fix:

  • Carry all possible supporting documents (age proof, address proof, photos) to satisfy specific official demands.

Typographical errors (spelling of names, wrong dates) in the final certificate, requiring further application for corrections.

How to fix:

  • Submit a correction application immediately through MeeSeva with a notarized affidavit.
